narrative.context: It’s late. You feel tired and irritable while trying to choose between two important options (course/project path).
narrative.prompt: How do you use your emotional state to support better thinking?

options.label: Use fatigue as a cue to postpone and structure
options.description: Recognize tiredness may bias choices. Write pros/cons and revisit in the morning with a clear plan.

ei.rationale: Uses emotion/physiological state as information to protect decision quality—facilitates thinking.

options.label: Ask someone else to decide for you
options.description: Hand over the decision to reduce stress.

ei.rationale: Reduces immediate discomfort, but doesn’t fully engage cognition; partial use of emotional information.

options.label: Decide quickly to end discomfort
options.description: Choose impulsively just to stop thinking about it.

ei.rationale: Emotion drives haste; thinking quality declines.

options.label: Ruminate for hours
options.description: Keep thinking in circles without structure.

ei.rationale: Emotion amplifies unproductive thinking; no facilitation of problem-solving.
